rulings.Case Title: HIRAM BORUNDA VS. MAKARIZO, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deposition Testimony Of Persons Most Qualified And Production Of Documents, Set One, And For Sanctions Against Defendant Brooks Brothers Group, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery on Friday, March 14, 2025 Line 8. PLAINTIFF HIRAM BORUNDA's Motion To Compel Deposition Testimony Of Persons Most Qualified And Production Of Documents, Set One, And For Sanctions Against Defendant Brooks Brothers Group, Inc. Off calendar. Parties failed to provide courtesy copies in compliance with SF Local Rule 2.7B. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: PARHAM BARGHCHI VS. GOOD LIVING SERVICES, INC., A CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Requesting Dismissal Of Entire Action
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery on Friday, March 14, 2025 Line 4. PLAINTIFF PARHAM BARGHCHI's Motion Requesting Dismissal Of Entire Action. Plaintiff Parham Barghchi's unopposed motion requesting dismissal of entire action is DENIED. Plaintiff moves under CCP section 581(b)(1) and Labor Code section 2699(l). Plaintiff seeks to dismiss individual and representative PAGA claims. The court construes Plaintiff's reference to Labor Code section 2699(l) (which itself is inapplicable to this motion) as a reference to section 2699(s), which, in fact, applies to motions to settle PAGA claims. "The superior court shall review and approve any settlement of any civil action filed pursuant to this part." (Lab. Code, section 2699(s)(2).) Here, Plaintiff effectively seeks judicial approval of a settlement of his individual and representative PAGA claims, but he has not provided a copy of the settlement for the court's review. The court will not approve a settlement it has not reviewed. Section 2699(s)(2) establishes another precondition to judicial approval of a PAGA settlement: "The proposed settlement shall be submitted to the agency at the same time that it is submitted to the court." Plaintiff here has not demonstrated that he submitted the proposed settlement to the LWDA. The court, thus, cannot approve the settlement and dismissal under section 2699(s)(2). Nor can or will the court approve dismissal under section 581(b)(1), given that the dismissal is based on settlement of the PAGA claims, which cannot be approved on this record. Plaintiff is advised that courtesy documents must be fastened; each filed document should be fastened as one. Going forward, stacks of loose paper, like the one received as "courtesy copies" on this motion, will be discarded and the motion will be disposed of as if no courtesy copies were lodged. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)
